entry.title: Review: KFC - Spicy Zinger Sandwich
entry.summary:
KFC's Spicy Zinger Sandwich features a new Extra Crispy Spicy Zinger marinated chicken breast filet served with shredded Romaine lettuce and the Colonel's Mayo on a toasted brioche bun. It's a test item here in California.
I bought one for $6.49. I think my local KFC was still working out the kinks on making this (or they just didn't have lettuce on hand), as mine came with pickles instead of lettuce. I didn't mind it as lettuce on a hot chicken sandwich tends to wilt quickly, while pickles add a nice crunch and acidity. In terms of texture, the new chicken filet felt the same as KFC's regular Extra Crispy chicken breast filet. Mine felt like it had been sitting in the warmer for a while, but it was thick, relatively tender, and moist inside and flaky and crispy outside.
Flavor-wise, there was a light seasoned flavor and a respectable amount of medium heat. It was spicy enough to get my nose running, but not so much that I went reaching for milk. It's probably as spicy as you're going to get at a major fast-food chain that isn't dishing Buffalo wings. The bun was a little stale. It was still moist inside, but the crust was somewhat hardened and dry. Overall, KFC's Spicy Zinger Sandwich was pretty good and even better if you can get a fresh one. I enjoyed the heat, and I prefer a spicy chicken filet over a spicy sauce. Note - Sorry, there's no cross-section shot for this review. I cut the sandwich and set up the shot, but then had a brain fart and didn't take a picture. Nutritional Info - KFC Spicy Zinger Sandwich Calories - 480 Fat - 23g (Saturated Fat - 3g) Sodium - 1360mg Carbs - 37g (Sugar - 5g) Protein - 33g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Little Caesars Launches New Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix
entry.summary:
Little Caesars introduces its new Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix as a limited-time offer that's a twist on the chain's regular-menu Slices-N-Stix.
Positioned as a single-serve meal, the Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix features two slices of Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza, four pieces of Italian Cheese Bread, and Crazy Sauce for dipping. You can consider it as either half a Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza that's cut and topped like Italian Cheese Bread on one half or as an order of Italian Cheese Bread that's cut and topped like a Detroit-Style Deep Dish Pepperoni pizza on one half. The Detroit-Style Slices-N-Stix is available now for $4.99 (it's $5.99 here in California). Photo via Little Caesars. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Bojangles Debuts New Breakfast Bowl and Brings Back Chicken Rice Bowl
entry.summary:
Bojangles rings in the new year with the introduction of its new Breakfast Bowl and the return of the Chicken Rice Bowl.
The new Breakfast Bowl is available all day and features country-style sausage, Bo-Rounds (fried potato coins similar to tater tots), eggs, Southern sausage gravy, and Monterey Jack cheese. The returning Chicken Rice Bowl features a bed of Bojangles Dirty Rice slow-cooked Cajun Pintos topped with a shredded Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheese blend and a choice of chicken (grilled chicken, two Chicken Supremes, or five BoBites), served with Texas Pete hot sauce on the side. Both the Breakfast Bowl and Chicken Rice Bowl are available now for a limited time at participating Bojangles locations. Prices vary, but a location in Atlanta, GA sells the Breakfast Bowl for $5.99 and the Chicken Rice Bowl for $7.49. Other limited-time specials at Bojangles right now include two-for-$4 Sausage Biscuits and the Salted Caramel Apple Crisp introduced last fall. Photo via Bojangles. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Pizza Hut Offers $10 Big New Yorker Deal
entry.summary:
Pizza Hut is running a limited-time deal where you can get its Big New Yorker pizza for $10 at participating locations nationwide.
The Big New Yorker is an extra-large (16-inch) New York-style pizza dusted with parmesan-oregano seasoning and cut into six foldable slices. For $10 ($10.99 here in Los Angeles), you can get it with one topping of your choice or as a Pepperoni Duo (topped with crispy cupped pepperoni and regular pepperoni). Additional toppings and extra cheese are available for an upcharge. Photo via Pizza Hut. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Fish Sandwiches are Back at Arby's
entry.summary:
Fish sandwiches make their annual return to Arby's for the 2026 Lenten season.
This year's lineup includes the Crispy Fish Sandwich and King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe. The Crispy Fish Sandwich is a classic fish sandwich consisting of a crispy-fried fish filet served with shredded lettuce and tartar sauce on a toasted sesame bun. A slice of cheddar and/or bacon can be added for an additional charge. The King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe features A crispy-fried fish filet with cheddar cheese, shredded lettuce, tomato, and tartar sauce on a toasted King's Hawaiian roll. Prices vary, but the Crispy Fish Sandwich costs $5.69 at a location here in Los Angeles, while the King's Hawaiian Fish Deluxe goes for $6.69. Other limited-time items at Arby's right now include the Italian Beef Dip, Apple Pie Shake, and Peach Cobbler Roll. The fast-food chain also has a limited-time, discounted combo meal called the "Meat & 3 Box." Photo via Arby's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Volcano Quesarito is Back at Taco Bell
entry.summary:
The Volcano Quesarito is back for a limited time at Taco Bell with a slight tweak: new Volcano Sauce. Additionally, the chain introduces the Rewards Member-exclusive Volcano Style Menu featuring various regular menu items with new Volcano Sauce added.
Taco Bell describes its new Volcano Sauce as a "creamy, craveable sauce made with a blend of cheese and flavorful spices, delivering the heat legacy of Lava Sauce that fans know and love." It's positioned as a "bold evolution of fan-favorite Lava Sauce." You can find it in the Volcano Quesarito and featured in the online-only Volcano Style Menu. Volcano Sauce can also be added to any menu item or ordered on the side. The Volcano Quesarito (here's my previous review from when it cost $2.69 and contained Lava Sauce instead of Volcano Sauce) features a grilled quesadilla with melted cheese, nacho cheese sauce, and new Volcano Sauce wrapped around a filling of seasoned ground beef, seasoned rice, and reduced-fat sour cream. It carries a suggested price of $4.99 (it's $5.49 at my local Taco Bell here in Los Angeles). The Volcano Style Menu can be found under the "Fan Style" tab on the Taco Bell website and mobile app when signed into your Taco Bell Rewards account. It includes: - Volcano Crunchy Taco - A Crunchy Taco with Volcano Sauce added. - Volcano Crunchwrap - A Crunchwrap Supreme with Volcano Sauce added. - Volcano Cheesy G - A Cheesy Gordita Crunch with Volcano Sauce instead of Spicy Ranch. - Volcano Steak Fries - Steak Garlic Nacho Fries with Volcano Sauce instead of Creamy Garlic Sauce. - Volcano BellGrande - Nachos Bell Grande with Volcano Sauce added. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Burger King Debuts New Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper and Fried Apple Pie
entry.summary:
Burger King introduces the new Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper, the fast-food chain's latest fan-inspired creation submitted through the "Whopper by You" platform, which encourages fans to submit their own Whopper ideas. Burger King also welcomes a new fried Cinnamon Apple Pie.
The Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper features a 4.4-oz flame-grilled beef patty served with crispy onion rings, bacon, sautéed mushrooms, Swiss cheese, and creamy peppercorn aioli on a toasted sesame seed bun. A Jr. version is also available. Prices for the new Whopper vary, but it goes for $10.89 at my local BK here in Los Angeles (the Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper Jr. costs $6.29). Burger King has used the "steakhouse" moniker for quite a few burgers in the past. Some examples include the Steakhouse Whopper, the Steakhouse King, and the BK Stuffed Steakhouse Burger. The new, limited-time Cinnamon Apple Pie is a deep-fried turnover-style pie with a crispy crust and warm, cinnamon apple filling. It costs $1.99 near me. The chain previously offered the Dutch Apple Pie, which was sold by the slice and discontinued in 2020. The Ultimate Steakhouse Whopper follows last year's BBQ Brisket Whopper and Crispy Onion Whopper in the "Whopper by You" series. Burger King plans to release additional guest-inspired Whopper burgers later this year. Photo via Burger King. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Dutch Bros Launches Three New Breakfast-Inspired Drinks
entry.summary:
Dutch Bros welcomes 2026 with the launch of three new breakfast-inspired drinks.
Available now for a limited time at all Dutch Bros locations, the drinks include: - Maple Waffle Latte - Featuring salted caramel, cinnamon, and white chocolate flavors, finished with Soft Top (sweet cold foam), a caramel drizzle, and raw sugar sprinkles, you can order it as an iced latte, iced protein latte, Freeze, or iced chai. - Banana Bread Mocha with Soft Top - Banana and hazelnut flavored, finished with Soft Top. You can get it as an iced mocha, mocha Freeze, or mocha cold brew. - Smooth Strawberry Rebel - A smoothie-inspired drink featuring strawberry and banana flavors mixed with Dutch Bros Rebel energy drink and sweet cream. You can order it as a blended or iced Rebel, or an iced lemonade. Photo via Dutch Bros. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Bakes New Oreo Cookies & Cream Sandwich Cookie and More Through January 17, 2026
entry.summary:
Crumbl's weekly rotating menu from January 12 to January 17, 2026, includes the new Cookies & Cream Sandwich Cookie featuring Oreo, along with three other cookies.
Here's what you can expect from this week's lineup: - Cookies & Cream Sandwich Cookie featuring Oreo - Two cookies & cream cookies sandwiching a layer of vanilla buttercream and cookies & cream crunch filling. - S'mores Brownie - A fudge brownie layered over a buttery graham cracker crust, topped with marshmallow buttercream, a drizzle of milk chocolate, and a graham cracker streusel. - Wedding Cake Cookie - A warm vanilla bean sugar cookie topped with swirled vanilla and raspberry cream cheese frosting and white chocolate pearls. - Blueberry Cheesecake Cookie - A chilled graham cracker cookie topped with cheesecake frosting and blueberry topping. As with last week, Crumbl offers a permanent Classic Menu. It is also offering Chocolate Chip Cookie Thins on Thursdays (they call it "Thin Thursdays"). Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: KFC Tests New Zinger Sandwich
entry.summary:
KFC is testing its new Zinger Sandwich at participating locations throughout California.
The new sandwich is meant to be a spicier, more flavorful version of the Zinger Sandwich launched (and rather quickly discontinued) in 2017. The new Zinger Sandwich features a "spicy marinated chicken breast with that Extra Crispy crunch, shredded lettuce, and creamy Colonel’s Mayo on a toasted brioche bun." Compared to the previous version, KFC has changed the recipe for the chicken breast filet and swapped out the sesame seed bun for the toasted brioche bun. It's interesting to note that, if it makes it out of testing, the Zinger Sandwich would be the only menu item containing lettuce on the KFC menu. Prices vary, but the sandwich costs $6.49 at a local KFC here in Los Angeles, which is the same price as the chain's Classic Chicken Sandwich. Photo via KFC. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Whataburger Welcomes New Whatadeals Value Menu
entry.summary:
Whataburger begins the new year with the launch of a new Whatadeal value menu with items priced at $3, $4, and $5. The lineup includes:
- $3 Big Ranch Wrap - A crispy-fried Whatachick’n Strip, lettuce, tomato, Creamy Buttermilk Ranch, and American cheese folded up in a warm tortilla. - $4 Whatachick’n Bites (5-piece) - Small, crispy-fried chicken breast pieces with gravy or other sauce for dipping. - $5 Bacon and Cheese Whataburger Jr. - A beef patty, bacon, American cheese, lettuce, tomato, pickles, diced onions, and mustard on a 4-inch bun. Photo via Whataburger. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Subway Introduces New Protein Pockets
entry.summary:
Subway introduces new Protein Pockets, which are wraps that offer over 20 grams of protein for $3.99 each. The sandwich chain also launches a new six-inch Sub of the Day for $4.99.
Available for a limited time, Protein Pockets come in four varieties: - Baja Chicken - Grilled chicken strips, Monterey cheddar, Baja Chipotle sauce, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and jalapenos, wrapped in a 9-inch tortilla and toasted. - Peppercorn Ranch Chicken - Grilled chicken strips, Monterey cheddar, Peppercorn Ranch, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and pickles, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. - Italian Trio - Black Forest ham, pepperoni, Genoa salami, Monterey cheddar, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and mayo, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. - Turkey & Ham - Oven-roasted turkey, Black Forest ham, Monterey cheddar, lettuce, Roma tomatoes, and mayo, wrapped and toasted in a 9-inch tortilla. The weekly schedule for the new $4.99 six-inch Sub of the Day is as follows: Additionally, you can get a drink, plus chips or cookies for $2 more. - Meatball Monday - Meatball Marinara - Tuna Tuesday - Classic Tuna - Sweet Onion Wednesday - Sweet Onion Chicken Teriyaki - Turkey Thursday - Oven-Roasted Turkey - Forest Ham Friday - Black Forest Ham - BMT Saturday - Italian B.M.T. - Spicy Italian Sunday - Spicy Italian Finally, Subway updated its MVP Rewards loyalty program to the new Sub Club loyalty program in December. The new program is reminiscent of the chain's past Sub Club sticker cards and offers a free Footlong after you buy three Footlong subs. In comparison, the Sub Club of old required eight stickers/stamps (they gave one sticker for buying a six-inch sub and two stickers for buying a Footlong sub) to redeem for a six-inch sub. Photo via Subway. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Korean-Style Menu Returns to Shake Shack with Two New Additions
entry.summary:
Shake Shack's Korean-Style Menu is back for a limited time and includes two new additions: K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites and the K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake.
The menu is available now when ordering online through the Shake Shack app or website ahead of a nationwide launch on January 9, 2026. K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites features crispy-fried white-meat chicken bites coated in a spicy-sweet sesame gochujang glaze and sprinkled with fresh scallions. They're served with your choice of dipping sauce. The K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake is a milkshake made with spicy gochujang caramel frozen custard topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of gochujang caramel. Returning Korean-Style Menu items include: - K-Shack Fried Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken breast filet glazed in spicy-sweet sesame gochujang and served with fresh scallions and kimchi slaw made with Choi’s Kimchi, on a toasted potato bun. - K-Shack BBQ Burger - A quarter-pound Angus beef patty (or two or three), American cheese, crispy-fried sweet onions, fresh scallions, and Korean BBQ sauce, served on a toasted potato bun. - K-Shack Spicy BBQ Fries - Crinkle-cut fries dusted with kimchi seasoning and served with a side of Korean BBQ sauce. Cheese Sauce can also be added for an additional charge. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!
